summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. It holds a collection of children behind the same interface as a leaf, so clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Composite because {{className}} pairs a child-management operation with a child container (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) while sharing the component interface — the part-whole shape.

composite_child_operation.note: Child management. add/remove on the same interface as the leaf is what lets clients ignore the part-whole distinction.

composite_child_container.note: The child collection. Holding children of the component type is the structural core of Composite.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Decorator: Decorator wraps exactly one component to add behaviour. Composite holds many children to model a tree — the container of children, not a single wrappee, is the difference.
